title: Intro to Frontend Frameworks Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the Frontend Framework role in structuring complex UIs, consistency/speed benefits, and the difference between libraries and frameworks.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primary reason frontend frameworks emerged?
options: To make websites look older.
To provide structure, tools, and best practices for building complex web applications with less code.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which and/or what is one of the biggest benefits of using frameworks like React, Vue, or Angular?
options: Consistent code structure across large teams.
They make the browser execute CSS faster.
They only work for mobile apps.
None of the above.

questions.question: What is the key difference between a library (like React) and a more opinionated framework (like Angular)?
options: Frameworks are for mobile and libraries are for web.
A Framework provides a more comprehensive, 'opinionated' solution for the whole app structure, while a Library is usually more focused on a single task (like the UI layer).
They are the same.
None of the above.

questions.question: Are 'Vanilla JS' and 'Framework JS' two different programming languages?
options: No, frameworks are built on top of the same JavaScript browser engines.
Yes, they use different compile steps and different logic.
Only for older versions.
None of the above.

questions.question: Does using a frontend framework allow you to focus more on your application's unique features instead of common infrastructure tasks?
options: Yes, as they provide robust, pre-built solutions for common problems (routing, state, etc).
No, it's always slower.
Only true for older versions.
None of the above.
